creator: kevans@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Bill Paul from comment #5)

> Also, regarding the other issue you run into with Mesa, I've noticed that with both 4.1.1 and my locally build 4.6.0 version, I sometimes see this:
> 
> D_CheckNetGame: Checking network game status.
> player 1 of 1 (1 nodes)
> Using video driver x11
> X connection to localhost:0 broken (explicit kill or server shutdown).
> 
> It's intermittent though: if I run it enough times it eventually works. It's not related to this issue though. (Hope you can eventually track it down.)

As I understand it, there's a patch proposed to mesa but it's kind of stalled. If you know the highest level of OpenGL you can run, you can specify -glversion to workaround it. e.g., `gzdoom -glversion 3.2`
